title: Position sizing: decide your risk BEFORE you click buy
_note: EVERGREEN (warm-up bank for ad traffic). No invented numbers/prices/dates.
body:
✅ #BeforeYouTrade — decide your risk BEFORE you click buy

Sizing is the one decision that has to be made cold, before the candle pulls you in. Get it right and a loss is a paper cut. Get it wrong and one trade can do real damage. Here's the order we work in.

☐ Fix your risk-per-trade FIRST. Decide the maximum you're willing to lose on this single position before anything else — as a fixed slice of your account, the same slice every time. This number is set when you're calm. It does not move because a setup 'feels' strong.

☐ Mark your SL from the chart, not from hope. Where is the trade simply wrong? That price is your invalidation. It comes from structure — not from how much you'd 'prefer' to lose.

☐ Size FROM the SL distance — never from feel. Your position size is whatever makes the distance between entry and SL equal to your fixed risk. Tight stop → larger size allowed. Wide stop → smaller size. The math sets the size. You don't.

☐ Choose leverage LAST. Leverage is an output of sizing, not the starting point. Pick the size first; leverage is just what's left over to express it. And the SL still has to sit before your liquidation price — always.

☐ One position can't sink you. If a single trade hitting its stop would hurt the account in a way you'd feel for days, the size is wrong. Cut it until the loss is boring.

Sizing from 'feel' is how good reads still blow accounts. Sizing from the SL is how we run aggressive leverage on purpose and survive the red ones. Decide the loss before the entry — every time.

#BeforeYouTrade
